pages.name: Home
pages.description: Homepage introducing the nonprofit and directing users quickly into high-impact actions like donating, sponsoring, volunteering, attending events, fundraising, and advocacy, with dynamic content loaded from the backend interfaces.
primary_functions: Explain mission, impact, and core child hunger relief focus in clear, simple terms
Highlight primary calls-to-action for donating, sponsoring a child, volunteering, attending events, fundraising, and advocacy
Surface urgent needs such as high-need volunteer events, upcoming runs/walks, and key campaigns like summer meals
Provide quick access links to key tasks including donations, sponsorship, events, and advocacy tools

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click primary 'Donate' CTA for a one-time gift
Click primary 'Give monthly' CTA
Click 'Sponsor a Child' CTA
Click 'Volunteer' CTA
Click an upcoming Run/Walk event teaser
Click 'Start a Fundraiser' CTA
Click 'Write your representative' or 'Take Action' CTA

access_methods.description: Directly via root URL or index.html

pages.name: Donate
pages.description: Central donation page for one-time and monthly gifts, including program-targeted and country-targeted options with fee coverage and scheduling controls.
primary_functions: Support one-time donations with a tab or toggle, including a featured programs list showing children fed per $10 and a 'Give Now' button for each program
Support monthly donations with a tab or toggle, including a country selector that displays child malnutrition percentages to guide targeting
Allow donors to enter a custom donation amount (e.g., $45 one-time, $25 monthly) and optionally cover processing fees via a checkbox
Provide scheduling options for recurring gifts, including selecting a monthly charge date such as the 15th
Offer an SMS updates opt-in with phone number entry for monthly donors
Collect credit/debit card and billing address details in a secure payment form and submit the donation
Confirm successful donations and clearly indicate which program or country the gift supports

incoming_params.description: Preselects donation tab, expected values 'one_time' or 'monthly'.
Program to preselect for a program-restricted one-time donation.
Country to preselect for monthly country-targeted donations.
Suggested donation amount to prefill in the amount field.
Optional donation type hint such as 'program_restricted' or 'country_targeted'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to Home' on the donation confirmation state
Click program name link from featured programs list

access_methods.description: Accessed with optional params to preselect tab, program, or country

pages.name: Our Work & Programs
pages.description: Overview of the organization’s work plus a filterable list of programs, including school meal programs in different regions. Supports switching between an overview narrative and a focused programs directory view.
primary_functions: Describe the main program areas (e.g., school meals, emergency food, maternal nutrition) and overall impact
List all programs in a card/grid format with key metrics like region, type, and children served
Provide filters for program type (including school meal programs), region (including Africa), and minimum children served (e.g., 50,000+)
Allow users to open detailed program pages from program cards
Link to donation options pre-focused on specific programs where appropriate

incoming_params.description: Controls initial view: 'overview' or 'programs'.
Pre-filters the programs list by program type code such as 'school_meal_program'.
Pre-filters the programs list to a region such as 'africa'.
Pre-filters the programs list by minimum childrenServedTotal (e.g., 50000).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a program card
Click 'Donate to this program' on a program card

access_methods.description: Optional query params set initial view and filters

pages.name: Program Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific program, including impact, stories, and an info kit request form, with a contextual link back into the donation flow.
primary_functions: Present detailed information about a program’s objectives, location, activities, and number of children served
Show impact metrics and stories that demonstrate outcomes for children and communities
Provide a 'Request Info Kit' form with delivery method options, including 'Postal mail + email'
Collect required contact information (full name, email, postal address) and submit info kit requests
Offer contextual calls-to-action such as 'Donate to this program' linking back to the donation flow with the program preselected

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the program whose details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Donate to this program' button
Click 'Back to all programs' link

access_methods.description: Linked from program cards and contextual CTAs

pages.name: Volunteer Opportunities
pages.description: Search and filter volunteer opportunities by location, date, day of week, and level of need, focused on actions like finding high-need weekend meal-packing shifts.
primary_functions: Provide a ZIP code search field (e.g., 94103) for locating nearby volunteer opportunities
Offer distance or radius controls (e.g., 20 miles) and apply them to search results
Include date filters such as 'Next month' and day-of-week filters like 'Saturday'
Allow filtering by need level, including a 'High need' option to prioritize urgent opportunities
Support sorting results by date (earliest first) and display events in cards with key details
Link each volunteer event card to a detailed event page with registration

incoming_params.description: Optional ZIP code to prefill the location field.
Optional radius in miles to preselect in distance filter.
Optional quick date range preset such as 'next_month'.
Initial day-of-week filter, e.g., 'saturday'.
Initial need level filter, e.g., 'high'.
Optional activityType filter such as 'meal_packing'.
Sort order, e.g., 'date_asc'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a volunteer opportunity card

access_methods.description: Optional query params pre-populate filters for deep links

pages.name: Volunteer Event Detail
pages.description: Detail and registration page for a specific volunteer event, such as a high-need weekend meal-packing shift.
primary_functions: Display full event information including date, time, location, need level, and activity description
Present any requirements or age limits for volunteers
Provide a registration form with fields for number of adult participants (e.g., '1'), names, and contact information
Submit volunteer registrations and show a confirmation state with event details and next steps

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the volunteer opportunity to display and register for.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to all opportunities' link after viewing or registering

access_methods.description: Linked from volunteer opportunity search results
Registration form posts via registerForVolunteerOpportunity interface

pages.name: Child Sponsorship Search
pages.description: Search and filter page for finding children to sponsor, enabling users to narrow by age, region (such as Central America), and waiting time.
primary_functions: Provide age range filters (e.g., 6 to 10 years) to narrow the list of children
Offer region/location filters including 'Central America' and apply them to results
Include sort options such as 'Longest waiting' or 'Days waiting – High to Low'
Display child profiles in a card layout showing basic info (age, country, waiting time) and a link to the profile
Direct users from a selected child card to the detailed child profile and sponsorship page

incoming_params.description: Optional minimum age to preselect in age filter.
Optional maximum age to preselect in age filter.
Initial region filter such as 'central_america'.
Sort option code like 'longest_waiting'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a child profile card

access_methods.description: Query params may preset filters for targeted sponsorship campaigns

pages.name: Child Profile & Sponsorship
pages.description: Detailed profile for an individual child with sponsorship signup, including a standard $39/month commitment flow.
primary_functions: Present detailed information about the child, including age, location, background, and waiting duration
Highlight that the child is within the chosen criteria (e.g., ages 6–10 in Central America, longest waiting)
Provide a clear 'Sponsor for $39/month' call-to-action and prefill the standard monthly amount where appropriate
Allow users to confirm or adjust the monthly sponsorship amount (e.g., ensure $39) and frequency settings
Collect sponsor contact and mailing information and any required payment details for the recurring sponsorship
Submit the sponsorship and present a confirmation with an overview of the commitment

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the child whose profile and sponsorship form should be shown.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Find another child' after sponsorship confirmation

access_methods.description: Linked from sponsorship search results and featured child campaigns
Sponsorship form is submitted via startChildSponsorship interface

pages.name: Events
pages.description: Filterable listing of events such as runs, walks, and community gatherings, including family-friendly 5K runs in specific cities and months.
primary_functions: Allow filtering of events by type (e.g., 'Run/Walk') to find running and walking fundraisers
Provide location selection (e.g., city dropdown including Chicago) for region-specific events
Offer date range filters (e.g., June 1 to June 30 of the current year) and apply them to results
Display event cards with titles, dates, locations, and clearly marked 'Family 5K' events
Allow sorting or easy scanning to identify the earliest qualifying event and link to its detail page

incoming_params.description: Initial event type filter such as 'run_walk'.
Initial city filter, e.g., 'Chicago'.
Start of date range (ISO date string) to prefilter events.
End of date range (ISO date string) to prefilter events.
Optional keyword filter such as 'Family 5K'.
Sort order (e.g., 'date_asc') for initial results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an event card (e.g., the earliest matching Family 5K)

access_methods.description: Query params can deep-link to filtered views (type, city, date range)

pages.name: Event Detail & Registration
pages.description: Detailed event information and multi-participant registration flow for fundraising events such as Family 5K runs.
primary_functions: Show detailed event information including description, exact date, location, and schedule
Provide a start time dropdown for selecting among available start times and choosing the earliest option
Offer a registration form that supports adding multiple participants with types (adult, child) and details such as names and ages
Submit event registrations and display a confirmation with event details and any follow-up instructions

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the fundraising event to show and allow registration for.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to all events' link

access_methods.description: Linked from the Events listing and from homepage teasers
Registration form submission via registerForFundraisingEvent interface

pages.name: Fundraising Campaigns
pages.description: Overview of peer-to-peer fundraising and list of active campaigns users can fundraise for, such as a 'Summer Meals 2025' campaign.
primary_functions: Explain how personal fundraising works and why it supports child hunger relief
List active fundraising campaigns (e.g., 'Summer Meals 2025') in card format with brief descriptions
Allow users to select a campaign, such as 'Summer Meals 2025', and navigate to its full campaign page
Highlight key seasonal or urgent campaigns to guide user choices

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a fundraising campaign card

access_methods.description: Campaign cards link to fundraising_campaign.html with campaignId parameter

pages.name: Fundraising Campaign Detail & Page Creation
pages.description: Campaign-specific page where users can create a personal fundraiser and receive a shareable link, including configuration for title, goal, story, and custom URL slug.
primary_functions: Display detailed information about the selected campaign (e.g., 'Summer Meals 2025') and its goals
Provide a 'Create Fundraising Page' button that reveals or focuses a form for building a personal fundraising page
Allow users to set a fundraising goal amount (e.g., $500) and enter a page title such as 'Alex's Summer Meals Fundraiser'
Include a story/description field requiring at least a short multi-sentence explanation (e.g., 40+ words) of why the campaign matters
Offer a custom URL ending field (e.g., 'alex-summer-meals-500') to define the personal fundraising page link
On form submission, create the personal fundraising page and transition to a confirmation view on the same page showing the new URL
Provide a 'Copy link' button next to the generated personal fundraiser URL to copy it for sharing

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the fundraising campaign, such as the 'Summer Meals 2025' campaign.
Optional human-readable slug for SEO-friendly URLs.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to all campaigns' link

access_methods.description: Linked from the Fundraising Campaigns listing and homepage features
Personal fundraising page form posts via createPersonalFundraisingPage interface

pages.name: Advocacy & Write Your Representative
pages.description: Advocacy hub including a tool to send letters to representatives on issues like school breakfast funding, with ZIP-based representative lookup and customizable letter templates.
primary_functions: Explain the organization’s advocacy priorities, including school breakfast funding for children
Provide a 'Write Your Representative' tool with a ZIP code field (e.g., 10001) to look up officials
Offer a topic selector including 'Increase school breakfast funding' or equivalent
Display a pre-written letter template with an editable first paragraph where users can add custom text (e.g., 20+ words about school breakfasts)
Allow users to choose delivery options such as 'Send email through this site' and 'Generate print-ready PDF'
Collect required sender information including full name, email, street address, city, and state
Submit the advocacy letter via selected delivery methods and present confirmation

incoming_params.description: Optional advocacy topic to preselect, such as 'increase_school_breakfast_funding'.
Optional ZIP code (e.g., '10001') to prefill in the ZIP lookup field.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Advocacy FAQs' link from this page

access_methods.description: Query params can deep-link into a specific advocacy topic or ZIP context
Letter form submission via sendAdvocacyLetter interface

pages.name: About Us
pages.description: Background information on the organization, mission, and impact, including leadership, partners, and financial transparency details to build donor trust.
primary_functions: Describe the organization’s history, mission, and approach to ending child hunger
Present leadership, partners, and high-level financial transparency information
Reinforce trust and credibility to support donations, sponsorships, and other actions

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Donate now' CTA embedded in About content

access_methods.description: Linked from other pages such as footer badges or mission callouts

pages.name: Contact Us
pages.description: Contact information and general inquiry form for the organization, enabling visitors to reach staff with questions about donations, programs, volunteering, and more.
primary_functions: Display contact details such as mailing address, phone number, and key email addresses
Provide a general contact form for questions or comments with fields for name, email, and message
Route users to additional resources (FAQ, specific program contacts) as needed

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View FAQs' link adjacent to the contact form

access_methods.description: Contact form submission via submitContactForm interface

pages.name: FAQ
pages.description: Frequently asked questions about donating, sponsoring, volunteering, events, fundraising, and advocacy, consolidated into searchable help content.
primary_functions: Answer common questions about one-time and monthly giving, including how funds are used
Explain child sponsorship details such as costs, communication, and duration
Provide answers related to volunteering, event participation (e.g., runs/walks), and peer-to-peer fundraising
Clarify advocacy actions and how letters to representatives are handled

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Still need help? Contact us' link

access_methods.description: Linked from Contact and Advocacy pages for additional help

pages.name: Privacy Policy
pages.description: Legal page explaining how user data is collected, stored, and used across donations, event registrations, sponsorships, advocacy tools, and communications.
primary_functions: Describe what personal information is collected through donations, registrations, and advocacy tools
Explain how data is used, stored, and protected, including SMS and email communications
Provide information on cookies, third-party services, and user rights regarding their data

access_methods.description: Linked from forms and consent notices

pages.name: Terms & Conditions
pages.description: Legal terms for using the website and participating in donations, sponsorships, events, fundraising campaigns, and other activities.
primary_functions: Set out conditions of use for the website and its content
Explain terms related to donations, recurring gifts, sponsorships, event registrations, and fundraising pages
Outline disclaimers, liability limitations, and governing law

access_methods.description: Linked from donation, registration, and fundraising forms

header_links.description: Make a one-time or monthly donation
Start a personal or team fundraiser
Get in touch with the organization
